title: Baltimore’s incredible crime-fighting success story
gist: Yglesias credits Baltimore's Mayor Brandon Scott with the largest crime drop among major US cities via a focused-deterrence Group Violence Reduction Strategy, argues the method is broadly replicable, and flags Baltimore's strong non-crime fundamentals as a setup for an in-migration flywheel.

insights.claim: Baltimore has posted the standout crime decline among major American cities, exceeding even the broad national improvement in homicides and shootings.
insights.speaker: Matthew Yglesias
insights.so_what: Signals Baltimore as a turnaround city; sustained safety gains could re-rate its real estate and investment appeal.

insights.claim: Baltimore skipped the 2020-2021 nationwide murder surge entirely, unlike most of America.
insights.speaker: Matthew Yglesias
insights.so_what: The divergence from the national pandemic-era crime spike suggests policy, not just cyclical factors, drove Baltimore's outcome.
insights.quote: Baltimore just skipped the entire murder surge that afflicted most of America in 2020 and 2021

insights.claim: Baltimore's crime drop was driven by focused deterrence via the Group Violence Reduction Strategy, an approach Yglesias argues is broadly replicable across other large cities.
insights.speaker: Matthew Yglesias
insights.so_what: If replicable, focused deterrence is a scalable urban-safety lever other mayors could copy, with knock-on effects for city economies.
insights.locator: the thing that really matters here is the results

insights.claim: Mayor Brandon Scott's euphemistic communications choices—saying 'gun violence' over 'crime' and avoiding the word 'gang'—have cost him the national credit and hero status the results warrant.
insights.speaker: Matthew Yglesias
insights.so_what: Messaging framing, not results, explains why a successful policy model stays under the radar; watch for it to spread once branded better.

insights.claim: Baltimore is uniquely hobbled by crime because its fundamentals on housing, jobs, and built environment are otherwise strong, so removing crime could unlock outsized upside.
insights.speaker: Matthew Yglesias
insights.so_what: Baltimore is a high-optionality bet where crime was the binding constraint; strong bones plus safety is the setup value investors watch for.
insights.locator: But Charm City is a place

insights.claim: A sustained low-crime spell in Baltimore could trigger a positive cycle of in-migration and investment that unlocks many new appealing neighborhoods and generates large gains.
insights.speaker: Matthew Yglesias
insights.so_what: The reflexive in-migration/investment loop is the mechanism for a Baltimore re-rating; track migration and housing data as the tell.
insights.quote: A sustained low-crime spell in Baltimore could easily set off a positive cycle of in-migration and investment
insights.locator: But Charm City is a place

insights.claim: Scott won in 2020 after corruption scandals rocked Baltimore's establishment and ran as a young progressive rather than a tough-on-crime candidate, which partly explains his lack of national press despite the results.
insights.speaker: Matthew Yglesias
insights.so_what: A progressive delivering hard crime reductions complicates the standard left/tough-on-crime binary; a signal that method matters more than posture.
insights.locator: On the politics, Scott is an interesting case
